channel.title: polymythcalendar — Seminar Schools
channel.description: Public lectures, conferences, workshops, readings, festivals, exhibitions and civic events across Toronto and Southern Ontario, Kingston, and Montréal. Upcoming events from polymythcalendar.

item.title: Department of Computer Science at TMU: Energy-Aware Agents 2026
item.description: Toronto Metropolitan University, Toronto — conference — Official TMU research-events listing identifies Energy-Aware Agents 2026 as a one-day workshop hosted by the Department of Computer Science at Toronto Metropolitan University.

item.title: Toronto Fringe Festival 2026
item.description: 27 stages across Toronto — festival — Toronto Fringe lists 123 shows on 27 stages across Toronto from June 30 to July 12, 2026.

item.title: Public Talk: Les trous noirs sous toutes leurs couleurs
item.description: McGill University, Montréal — talk — Official McGill Faculty of Science events page lists Public Talk: Les trous noirs sous toutes leurs couleurs on June 30, 2026.

item.description: Aga Khan Museum — festival — Canada Day programming.

item.title: CFP: Toronto Workshop on Moral Psychology and Moral Theory
item.description: Department of Philosophy, University of Toronto, JHB, 170 St George St — cfp — Submission deadline. Workshop Nov 7-8, 2026. Topics: Philosophy of Cognitive Science, Value Theory.

item.title: International Studies Association 2027 Convention
item.description: Special-program deadlines June and July, confirm the general call. International relations. Atlanta, March 2027, with a virtual option for Global South scholars. — cfp — Special-program deadlines June and July, confirm the general call. International relations. Atlanta, March 2027, with a virtual option for Global South scholars.

item.title: Journal of Humanistic Psychology, Authenticity in Lived-Experience Work
item.description: Abstract deadline, July 1. Special issue on authenticity in peer and lived-experience workforces. Sage. Journal special issue. — cfp — Abstract deadline, July 1. Special issue on authenticity in peer and lived-experience workforces. Sage. Journal special issue.

item.title: Brott Music Festival 2026
item.description: Hamilton and Burlington venues — festival — Brott Music Festival’s 2026 season presents orchestral, opera, and popular-music concerts in Hamilton and Burlington.

item.title: Aliya Kanani: A Comedian’s Guide To Enlightenment
item.description: Tarragon Theatre Mainspace, 30 Bridgman Ave, Toronto — performance — Toronto Fringe performance by Aliya Kanani, with performances July 2–12, 2026.

item.title: National Academy Orchestra of Canada, Martin MacDonald, and Jim Witter: The Piano Men
item.description: Theatre Aquarius, Hamilton — performance — An orchestral evening of Elton John and Billy Joel hits, July 2, 2026 at 7:30 pm.

item.title: Saint Levant at Festival International de Jazz de Montréal 2026
item.description: TD Stage, Montréal — performance — Official Montréal Jazz Festival programme page lists Saint Levant on July 2, 2026 at the TD Stage.

item.description: Submission deadline (conference: Boston, Jan 13–16 2027) — cfp — Abstract deadline for the IAPT session at the 2027 Eastern APA.

item.title: Patrick Watson at Festival International de Jazz de Montréal 2026
item.description: TD Stage, Montréal — performance — Official Montréal Jazz Festival programme page lists Patrick Watson on July 3, 2026 at the TD Stage.

item.title: CFP: University of Toronto Philosophy Graduate Conference 2026
item.description: Department of Philosophy, University of Toronto, JHB, 170 St George St — cfp — Submission deadline 11:59pm EST. Conference Oct 16-17, 2026. Keynotes Don Garrett (NYU), Alva Noe (UC Berkeley).

item.description: Aga Khan Museum — gathering

item.title: Write the World Monthly Competitions
item.description: Monthly competitions, a new genre each month, opening the first Monday. Poetry, fiction, journalism, screenwriting, and more. Open worldwide, ages 13 to 19. Free. Cash prizes and expert review. — contest — Monthly competitions, a new genre each month, opening the first Monday. Poetry, fiction, journalism, screenwriting, and more. Open worldwide, ages 13 to 19. Free. Cash prizes and expert review.

item.title: The Impressionist Revolution: Monet to Matisse
item.description: Art Gallery of Ontario — exhibition — Members preview June 24; public July 7. 50 works organized by the Dallas Museum of Art; Canadian debut.

item.description: Young Centre for the Performing Arts — performance — Soulpepper 2026/27.

item.description: Concordia University, Montréal — workshop — Official Concordia GradProSkills event page for a workshop on funding opportunities for graduate students in social sciences, arts and humanities research programs.

item.description: L.R. Wilson Hall, McMaster University, Hamilton — performance — Opera arias, duets, and ensembles, July 9, 2026 at 7:30 pm.

item.description: FOFA Gallery, Concordia University, Montréal — exhibition — Official Concordia FOFA Gallery event page lists The Let Down Reflex exhibition running from July 10 to August 21, 2026.

item.title: PAMLA 2026 Conference
item.description: Panel submission window, projected, verify. Literature and languages. Seattle, November 2026. Pacific Ancient and Modern Language Association. Confirm on the portal. — cfp — Panel submission window, projected, verify. Literature and languages. Seattle, November 2026. Pacific Ancient and Modern Language Association. Confirm on the portal.

item.title: Department of Philosophy, University of Toronto, with the Ontario Philosophy Teachers' Association: The Aristotle Contest
item.description: Canada-wide high-school philosophy essay contest — contest — Verified 2026 deadline. Canadian high-school students submit one philosophy essay of up to 1,200 words on one of the official questions. English and French submissions are welcome. Cash prizes are awarded for first, second, and third place, with honourable mentions.

item.title: Fantasia International Film Festival 2026
item.description: Concordia Hall, J.A. de Sève cinemas, and Cinéma du Musée, Montréal — festival — Fantasia’s 30th edition runs July 16 through August 2, 2026, with screenings, workshops, and launch events in Montréal.

item.title: National Academy Orchestra of Canada, Tania Miller, and Dana Fradkin: La Bohème
item.description: Theatre Aquarius, Hamilton — performance — A fully staged production of Puccini’s La bohème, July 16 and 18, 2026 at 7:30 pm.

item.title: Jane Schoenbrun and Hannah Einbinder: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma — Canadian Premiere and Q&A
item.description: SGW Alumni Auditorium, Montréal — screening — Fantasia lists the Canadian premiere for July 17, 2026, with Jane Schoenbrun and Hannah Einbinder in attendance for a special Q&A.

item.title: Hillside Festival 2026
item.description: Guelph Lake Conservation Area, 7743 Conservation Rd, Guelph — festival-of-form — Three-day music festival at Guelph Lake Island. Shuttle from Guelph downtown.
